The event will take place in San Francisco, California, United States."},{"url":"https://climatwin.com/sf-climate-week-2026-9-days-700-events-1000-organizations-and-25k-attendees-in-the-bay-area/","title":"SF Climate Week 2026: 9 Days, 700+ Events, 1,000+ Organizations, And 25K+ Attendees In The Bay Area - ClimaTwin","content":"Feb 23, 2026 — SF Climate Week 2026 (April 18–26) is a decentralized Bay Area “open calendar” with 700+ events, 1000+ organizations, ...
SF Climate Week will run from April 18–26, 2026, as a decentralized Bay Area “open calendar” convening led by Climatebase in partnership with the City of San Francisco and other collaborators (SF Climate Week, 2026c).
Organizers report a scale of more than 700 events, participation from over 1,000 organizations, and more than 25,000 attendees, reflecting rapid expansion from 2024 participation levels and 2025 projections (Climatebase Team, 2025a; Ellis, 2025; SF Climate Week, 2026c).
Organizers indicate that event submissions will open in February 2026 and close in early April 2026, providing a defined window for organizations seeking to host sessions aligned to the official calendar and tracks (SF Climate Week, 2026a).
Climatebase also reports planned distribution through its owned channels, including an email newsletter audience and LinkedIn following, with optional promotional offerings available for hosts seeking increased visibility within the calendar (SF Climate Week, 2026a).
Executive Summary A February 2026 report covered by Green Central Banking argues that many climate-economy models used by governments, central banks, and investors understate physical climate risk because they lean heavily on GDP and global mean temperature,...
Crossroads is a hybrid event hosted by the National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine that connects two high-signal climate priorities for practitioners and policymakers: the science information landscape and locally..."},{"url":"https://www.changeclimate.org/blog/sf-climate-week-2026","title":"The Movement is dead, but movement is everywhere: Notes from SF Climate Week 2026 | Change Climate","content":"To attend a “climate week” is to immerse yourself in a city brimming with climate-themed events and overdose yourself with FOMO."}]}
